chapter_title.en: Chapter 92 – The perfume for the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح موقوف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/312)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح موقوف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/312)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَأْكُلُ الْأُتْرُجَّ قَالَ نَعَمْ قُلْتُ لَهُ رَائِحَةٌ طَيِّبَةٌ قَالَ الْأُتْرُجُّ طَعَامٌ لَيْسَ هُوَ مِنَ الطِّيبِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Ahmad Bin Al Hassan, from Amro Bin Saeed, from Musaddaq Bin Sadaqa, from Ammar Bin Musa,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in Ihraam eating the citron. Heasws said: ‘Yes’. I said to himasws, ‘Its smell is aromatic’. Heasws said: ‘The citron is food. It is not from the perfumes’.
en.sarwar: 16. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ahmad ibn al-Hassan from ‘Amr ibn Sa‘id from Musaddiq ibn Sadaqah from ‘Ammar ibn Musa who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram and eats citron. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can eat.’ I then said, ‘It has nice fragrance.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Citron is food and it is not a fragrance.’”
